alt.description: A description of the image used by assistive devices.

play.description: Plays the animation. When this attribute is remove, the animation will pause.

src.description: The path to the image to load.

wa-error.description: Emitted when the image fails to load.

wa-load.description: Emitted when the image loads successfully.

slots.pause-icon: Optional pause icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.play-icon: Optional play icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

delay.description: The number of milliseconds to delay the start of the animation.

direction.description:
Determines the direction of playback as well as the behavior when reaching the end of an iteration.
[Learn more](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/animation-direction)

duration.description: The number of milliseconds each iteration of the animation takes to complete.

easing.description:
The easing function to use for the animation. This can be a Web Awesome easing function or a custom easing function
such as `cubic-bezier(0, 1, .76, 1.14)`.

end-delay.description: The number of milliseconds to delay after the active period of an animation sequence.

fill.description: Sets how the animation applies styles to its target before and after its execution.

iterations.description: The number of iterations to run before the animation completes. Defaults to `Infinity`, which loops.

iteration-start.description: The offset at which to start the animation, usually between 0 (start) and 1 (end).

name.description: The name of the built-in animation to use. For custom animations, use the `keyframes` prop.

play.description:
Plays the animation. When omitted, the animation will be paused. This attribute will be automatically removed when
the animation finishes or gets canceled.

playback-rate.description:
Sets the animation's playback rate. The default is `1`, which plays the animation at a normal speed. Setting this
to `2`, for example, will double the animation's speed. A negative value can be used to reverse the animation. This
value can be changed without causing the animation to restart.

wa-cancel.description: Emitted when the animation is canceled.

wa-start.description: Emitted when the animation starts or restarts.

slots.(default): The element to animate. Avoid slotting in more than one element, as subsequent ones will be ignored. To animate multiple elements, either wrap them in a single container or use multiple `<wa-animation>` elements.

cancel.description: Clears all keyframe effects caused by this animation and aborts its playback.

finish.description: Sets the playback time to the end of the animation corresponding to the current playback direction.

image.description: The image source to use for the avatar.

initials.description: Initials to use as a fallback when no image is available (1-2 characters max recommended).

label.description: A label to use to describe the avatar to assistive devices.

loading.description: Indicates how the browser should load the image.

wa-error.description: The image could not be loaded. This may because of an invalid URL, a temporary network condition, or some unknown cause.

slots.icon: The default icon to use when no image or initials are present. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

attention.description: Adds an animation to draw attention to the badge.

pill.description: Draws a pill-style badge with rounded edges.

variant.description: The badge's theme variant. Defaults to `brand` if not within another element with a variant.

label.description:
The label to use for the breadcrumb control. This will not be shown on the screen, but it will be announced by
screen readers and other assistive devices to provide more context for users.

slots.(default): One or more breadcrumb items to display.
slots.separator: The separator to use between breadcrumb items. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

href.description:
Optional URL to direct the user to when the breadcrumb item is activated. When set, a link will be rendered
internally. When unset, a button will be rendered instead.

rel.description: The `rel` attribute to use on the link. Only used when `href` is set.

target.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is set.

slots.end: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed after the label.
slots.separator: The separator to use for the breadcrumb item. This will only change the separator for this item. If you want to change it for all items in the group, set the separator on `<wa-breadcrumb>` instead.
slots.start: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed before the label.

disabled.description: Disables the button. Does not apply to link buttons.

download.description: Tells the browser to download the linked file as this filename. Only used when `href` is present.

form.description:
The "form owner" to associate the button with. If omitted, the closest containing form will be used instead. The
value of this attribute must be an id of a form in the same document or shadow root as the button.

formaction.description: Used to override the form owner's `action` attribute.

formenctype.description: Used to override the form owner's `enctype` attribute.

formmethod.description: Used to override the form owner's `method` attribute.

formnovalidate.description: Used to override the form owner's `novalidate` attribute.

formtarget.description: Used to override the form owner's `target` attribute.

href.description: When set, the underlying button will be rendered as an `<a>` with this `href` instead of a `<button>`.

loading.description: Draws the button in a loading state.

name.description:
The name of the button, submitted as a name/value pair with form data, but only when this button is the submitter.
This attribute is ignored when `href` is present.

pill.description: Draws a pill-style button with rounded edges.

rel.description: When using `href`, this attribute will map to the underlying link's `rel` attribute.

target.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is present.

type.description:
The type of button. Note that the default value is `button` instead of `submit`, which is opposite of how native
`<button>` elements behave. When the type is `submit`, the button will submit the surrounding form.

value.description:
The value of the button, submitted as a pair with the button's name as part of the form data, but only when this
button is the submitter. This attribute is ignored when `href` is present.

variant.description: The button's theme variant. Defaults to `neutral` if not within another element with a variant.

with-caret.description: Draws the button with a caret. Used to indicate that the button triggers a dropdown menu or similar behavior.

blur.description: Emitted when the button loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the button gains focus.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.end: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed after the label.
slots.start: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed before the label.

click.description: Simulates a click on the button.

focus.description: Sets focus on the button.

label.description:
A label to use for the button group. This won't be displayed on the screen, but it will be announced by assistive
devices when interacting with the control and is strongly recommended.

variant.description: The button group's theme variant. Defaults to `neutral` if not within another element with a variant.

slots.(default): One or more `<wa-button>` elements to display in the button group.

variant.description: The callout's theme variant. Defaults to `brand` if not within another element with a variant.

slots.icon: An icon to show in the callout. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

with-footer.description: Renders the card with a footer. Only needed for SSR, otherwise is automatically added.

with-header.description: Renders the card with a header. Only needed for SSR, otherwise is automatically added.

with-media.description: Renders the card with an image. Only needed for SSR, otherwise is automatically added.

slots.actions: An optional actions section to render at the end for the horizontal card.
slots.footer: An optional footer for the card.
slots.footer-actions: An optional actions section to render in the footer of the vertical card.
slots.header: An optional header for the card.
slots.header-actions: An optional actions section to render in the header of the vertical card.
slots.media: An optional media section to render at the start of the card.

autoplay.description: When set, the slides will scroll automatically when the user is not interacting with them.

autoplay-interval.description: Specifies the amount of time, in milliseconds, between each automatic scroll.

loop.description: When set, allows the user to navigate the carousel in the same direction indefinitely.

mouse-dragging.description: When set, it is possible to scroll through the slides by dragging them with the mouse.

navigation.description: When set, show the carousel's navigation.

orientation.description: Specifies the orientation in which the carousel will lay out.

pagination.description: When set, show the carousel's pagination indicators.

slides-per-move.description:
Specifies the number of slides the carousel will advance when scrolling, useful when specifying a `slides-per-page`
greater than one. It can't be higher than `slides-per-page`.

slides-per-page.description: Specifies how many slides should be shown at a given time.

wa-slide-change.description: Emitted when the active slide changes.

slots.(default): The carousel's main content, one or more `<wa-carousel-item>` elements.
slots.next-icon: Optional next icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.previous-icon: Optional previous icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

goToSlide.description: Scrolls the carousel to the slide specified by `index`.

next.description: Move the carousel forward by `slides-per-move` slides.

previous.description: Move the carousel backward by `slides-per-move` slides.

form.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

format.description:
The format to use. If opacity is enabled, these will translate to HEXA, RGBA, HSLA, and HSVA respectively. The color
picker will accept user input in any format (including CSS color names) and convert it to the desired format.

hint.description: The color picker's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

label.description:
The color picker's label. This will not be displayed, but it will be announced by assistive devices. If you need to
display HTML, you can use the `label` slot` instead.

name.description: The name of the form control, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

opacity.description: Shows the opacity slider. Enabling this will cause the formatted value to be HEXA, RGBA, or HSLA.

open.description:
Indicates whether or not the popup is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the popup, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the popup's open state.

required.description: Makes the color picker a required field.

size.description: Determines the size of the color picker's trigger

swatches.description:
One or more predefined color swatches to display as presets in the color picker. Can include any format the color
picker can parse, including HEX(A), RGB(A), HSL(A), HSV(A), and CSS color names. Each color must be separated by a
semicolon (`;`). Alternatively, you can pass an array of color values to this property using JavaScript.

uppercase.description: By default, values are lowercase. With this attribute, values will be uppercase instead.

value.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

without-format-toggle.description: Removes the button that lets users toggle between format.

blur.description: Emitted when the color picker loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the color picker receives focus.

change.description: Emitted when the color picker's value changes.

input.description: Emitted when the color picker receives input.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.hint: The color picker's form hint. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.
slots.label: The color picker's form label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

blur.description: Removes focus from the color picker.

focus.description: Sets focus on the color picker.

getFormattedValue.description: Returns the current value as a string in the specified format.

getHexString.signature: getHexString(hue: number, saturation: number, brightness: number, alpha = 100) => void
getHexString.description: Generates a hex string from HSV values. Hue must be 0-360. All other arguments must be 0-100.

reportValidity.description: Checks for validity and shows the browser's validation message if the control is invalid.

position.description: The position of the divider as a percentage.

slots.after: The after content, often an `` or `
slots.before: The before content, often an `` or `
slots.handle: The icon used inside the handle.

copy-label.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip.

error-label.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip when a copy error occurs.

feedback-duration.description: The length of time to show feedback before restoring the default trigger.

from.description:
An id that references an element in the same document from which data will be copied. If both this and `value` are
present, this value will take precedence. By default, the target element's `textContent` will be copied. To copy an
attribute, append the attribute name wrapped in square brackets, e.g. `from="el[value]"`. To copy a property,
append a dot and the property name, e.g. `from="el.value"`.

success-label.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip after copying.

tooltip-placement.description: The preferred placement of the tooltip.

wa-copy.description: Emitted when the data has been copied.

wa-error.description: Emitted when the data could not be copied.

slots.copy-icon: The icon to show in the default copy state. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.error-icon: The icon to show when a copy error occurs. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.success-icon: The icon to show when the content is copied. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

disabled.description: Disables the details so it can't be toggled.

icon-placement.description: The location of the expand/collapse icon.

name.description: Groups related details elements. When one opens, others with the same name will close.

open.description:
Indicates whether or not the details is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the details, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the details' open state.

summary.description: The summary to show in the header. If you need to display HTML, use the `summary` slot instead.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the details closes and all animations are complete.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the details opens and all animations are complete.

slots.collapse-icon: Optional collapse icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.expand-icon: Optional expand icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.summary: The details' summary. Alternatively, you can use the `summary` attribute.

label.description:
The dialog's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label, as it is required for
proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

light-dismiss.description: When enabled, the dialog will be closed when the user clicks outside of it.

open.description: Indicates whether or not the dialog is open. Toggle this attribute to show and hide the dialog.

without-header.description: Disables the header. This will also remove the default close button.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the dialog closes and all animations are complete.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the dialog opens and all animations are complete.

wa-hide.description: Emitted when the dialog is requested to close. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent the dialog from closing. You can inspect `event.detail.source` to see which element caused the dialog to close. If the source is the dialog element itself, the user has pressed [[Escape]] or the dialog has been closed programmatically. Avoid using this unless closing the dialog will result in destructive behavior such as data loss.

slots.footer: The dialog's footer, usually one or more buttons representing various options.
slots.header-actions: Optional actions to add to the header. Works best with `<wa-button>`.
slots.label: The dialog's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

label.description:
The drawer's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label, as it is required for
proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

light-dismiss.description: When enabled, the drawer will be closed when the user clicks outside of it.

open.description: Indicates whether or not the drawer is open. Toggle this attribute to show and hide the drawer.

placement.description: The direction from which the drawer will open.

without-header.description: Disables the header. This will also remove the default close button.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the drawer closes and all animations are complete.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the drawer opens and all animations are complete.

wa-hide.description: Emitted when the drawer is requesting to close. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent the drawer from closing. You can inspect `event.detail.source` to see which element caused the drawer to close. If the source is the drawer element itself, the user has pressed [[Escape]] or the drawer has been closed programmatically. Avoid using this unless closing the drawer will result in destructive behavior such as data loss.

slots.footer: The drawer's footer, usually one or more buttons representing various options.
slots.header-actions: Optional actions to add to the header. Works best with `<wa-button>`.
slots.label: The drawer's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

distance.description: The distance of the dropdown menu from its trigger.

placement.description:
The placement of the dropdown menu in reference to the trigger. The menu will shift to a more optimal location if
the preferred placement doesn't have enough room.

skidding.description: The offset of the dropdown menu along its trigger.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the dropdown has been hidden.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the dropdown has been shown.

wa-hide.description: Emitted when the dropdown is about to hide.

wa-select.description: Emitted when an item in the dropdown is selected.

wa-show.description: Emitted when the dropdown is about to show.

slots.trigger: The element that triggers the dropdown, such as a `<wa-button>` or `<button>`.

checked.description: Set to true to check the dropdown item. Only valid when `type` is `checkbox`.

submenuOpen.description: Whether the submenu is currently open.

type.description: Set to `checkbox` to make the item a checkbox.

value.description:
An optional value for the menu item. This is useful for determining which item was selected when listening to the
dropdown's `wa-select` event.

variant.description: The type of menu item to render.

blur.description: Emitted when the dropdown item loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the dropdown item gains focus.

slots.details: Additional content or details to display after the label.
slots.icon: An optional icon to display before the label.
slots.submenu: Submenu items, typically `<wa-dropdown-item>` elements, to create a nested menu.

display.description: Determines how to display the result, e.g. "100 bytes", "100 b", or "100b".

unit.description: The type of unit to display.

value.description: The number to format in bytes.

date.description:
The date/time to format. If not set, the current date and time will be used. When passing a string, it's strongly
recommended to use the ISO 8601 format to ensure timezones are handled correctly. To convert a date to this format
in JavaScript, use [`date.toISOString()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toISOString).

day.description: The format for displaying the day.

era.description: The format for displaying the era.

hour.description: The format for displaying the hour.

hour-format.description: The format for displaying the hour.

minute.description: The format for displaying the minute.

month.description: The format for displaying the month.

second.description: The format for displaying the second.

time-zone.description: The time zone to express the time in.

time-zone-name.description: The format for displaying the time.

weekday.description: The format for displaying the weekday.

year.description: The format for displaying the year.

currency.description: The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) currency code to use when formatting.

maximum-fraction-digits.description: The maximum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are 0-100.

maximum-significant-digits.description: The maximum number of significant digits to use,. Possible values are 1-21.

minimum-fraction-digits.description: The minimum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are 0-100.

minimum-integer-digits.description: The minimum number of integer digits to use. Possible values are 1-21.

minimum-significant-digits.description: The minimum number of significant digits to use. Possible values are 1-21.

form.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

hint.description: The checkbox's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

checked.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

indeterminate.description:
Draws the checkbox in an indeterminate state. This is usually applied to checkboxes that represents a "select
all/none" behavior when associated checkboxes have a mix of checked and unchecked states.

name.description: The name of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

required.description: Makes the checkbox a required field.

value.description: The value of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

blur.description: Emitted when the checkbox loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the checkbox gains focus.

change.description: Emitted when the checked state changes.

input.description: Emitted when the checkbox receives input.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the checkbox. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.

click.description: Simulates a click on the checkbox.

focus.description: Sets focus on the checkbox.

auto-width.description: Sets the width of the icon to match the cropped SVG viewBox. This operates like the Font `fa-width-auto` class.

family.description:
The family of icons to choose from. For Font Awesome Free, valid options include `classic` and `brands`. For
Font Awesome Pro subscribers, valid options include, `classic`, `sharp`, `duotone`, `sharp-duotone`, and `brands`.
A valid kit code must be present to show pro icons via CDN. You can set `<html data-fa-kit-code="...">` to provide
one.

label.description:
An alternate description to use for assistive devices. If omitted, the icon will be considered presentational and
ignored by assistive devices.

library.description: The name of a registered custom icon library.

name.description: The name of the icon to draw. Available names depend on the icon library being used.

src.description:
An external URL of an SVG file. Be sure you trust the content you are including, as it will be executed as code and
can result in XSS attacks.

swap-opacity.description: Swaps the opacity of duotone icons.

variant.description:
The name of the icon's variant. For Font Awesome, valid options include `thin`, `light`, `regular`, and `solid` for
the `classic` and `sharp` families. Some variants require a Font Awesome Pro subscription. Custom icon libraries
may or may not use this property.

wa-error.description: Emitted when the icon fails to load due to an error. When using `spriteSheet: true` this will not emit.

wa-load.description: Emitted when the icon has loaded. When using `spriteSheet: true` this will not emit.

allow-scripts.description:
Allows included scripts to be executed. Be sure you trust the content you are including as it will be executed as
code and can result in XSS attacks.

src.description:
The location of the HTML file to include. Be sure you trust the content you are including as it will be executed as
code and can result in XSS attacks.

wa-include-error.description: Emitted when the included file fails to load due to an error.

wa-load.description: Emitted when the included file is loaded.

autocapitalize.description: Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered by the user.

autocomplete.description:
Specifies what permission the browser has to provide assistance in filling out form field values. Refer to
[this page on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete) for available values.

autocorrect.description: Indicates whether the browser's autocorrect feature is on or off.

autofocus.description: Indicates that the input should receive focus on page load.

enterkeyhint.description: Used to customize the label or icon of the Enter key on virtual keyboards.

form.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

hint.description: The input's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

inputmode.description:
Tells the browser what type of data will be entered by the user, allowing it to display the appropriate virtual
keyboard on supportive devices.

label.description: The input's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

max.description: The input's maximum value. Only applies to date and number input types.

maxlength.description: The maximum length of input that will be considered valid.

min.description: The input's minimum value. Only applies to date and number input types.

minlength.description: The minimum length of input that will be considered valid.

password-toggle.description: Adds a button to toggle the password's visibility. Only applies to password types.

password-visible.description: Determines whether or not the password is currently visible. Only applies to password input types.

pattern.description: A regular expression pattern to validate input against.

pill.description: Draws a pill-style input with rounded edges.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the input is empty.

required.description: Makes the input a required field.

spellcheck.description: Enables spell checking on the input.

step.description:
Specifies the granularity that the value must adhere to, or the special value `any` which means no stepping is
implied, allowing any numeric value. Only applies to date and number input types.

type.description:
The type of input. Works the same as a native `<input>` element, but only a subset of types are supported. Defaults
to `text`.

value.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

with-clear.description: Adds a clear button when the input is not empty.

with-hint.description: Used for SSR. Will determine if the SSRed component will have the hint slot rendered on initial paint.

with-label.description: Used for SSR. Will determine if the SSRed component will have the label slot rendered on initial paint.

without-spin-buttons.description: Hides the browser's built-in increment/decrement spin buttons for number inputs.

blur.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

change.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user.

input.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

wa-clear.description: Emitted when the clear button is activated.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.clear-icon: An icon to use in lieu of the default clear icon.
slots.end: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed at the end of the input control.
slots.hide-password-icon: An icon to use in lieu of the default hide password icon.
slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.
slots.label: The input's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.
slots.show-password-icon: An icon to use in lieu of the default show password icon.
slots.start: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed at the start of the input control.

focus.description: Sets focus on the input.

select.description: Selects all the text in the input.

setRangeText.signature: setRangeText(replacement: string, start?: number, end?: number, selectMode: 'select' | 'start' | 'end' | 'preserve' = 'preserve') => void
setRangeText.description: Replaces a range of text with a new string.

setSelectionRange.signature: setSelectionRange(selectionStart: number, selectionEnd: number, selectionDirection: 'forward' | 'backward' | 'none' = 'none') => void
setSelectionRange.description: Sets the start and end positions of the text selection (0-based).

showPicker.description: Displays the browser picker for an input element (only works if the browser supports it for the input type).

stepDown.description: Decrements the value of a numeric input type by the value of the step attribute.

stepUp.description: Increments the value of a numeric input type by the value of the step attribute.

intersect-class.description:
CSS class applied to elements during intersection. Automatically removed when elements leave
the viewport, enabling pure CSS styling based on visibility state.

once.description: If enabled, observation ceases after initial intersection.

root.description: Element ID to define the viewport boundaries for tracked targets.

root-margin.description: Offset space around the root boundary. Accepts values like CSS margin syntax.

threshold.description: One or more space-separated values representing visibility percentages that trigger the observer callback.

wa-intersect.description: Fired when a tracked element begins or ceases intersecting.

slots.(default): Elements to track. Only immediate children of the host are monitored.

attr.description:
Watches for changes to attributes. To watch only specific attributes, separate them by a space, e.g.
`attr="class id title"`. To watch all attributes, use `*`.

attr-old-value.description: Indicates whether or not the attribute's previous value should be recorded when monitoring changes.

char-data.description: Watches for changes to the character data contained within the node.

char-data-old-value.description: Indicates whether or not the previous value of the node's text should be recorded.

child-list.description: Watches for the addition or removal of new child nodes.

slots.(default): The content to watch for mutations.

disabled.description: Draws the option in a disabled state, preventing selection.

label.description:
The optionâ€™s plain text label.
Usually automatically generated, but can be useful to provide manually for cases involving complex content.

value.description:
The option's value. When selected, the containing form control will receive this value. The value must be unique
from other options in the same group. Values may not contain spaces, as spaces are used as delimiters when listing
multiple values.

slots.end: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed after the label.
slots.start: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed before the label.

disable-navigation-toggle.description:
Determines whether or not to hide the default hamburger button.
This will automatically flip to "true" if you add an element with `data-toggle-nav` anywhere in the element light DOM.
Generally this will be set for you and you don't need to do anything, unless you're using SSR, in which case you should set this manually for initial page loads.

mobile-breakpoint.description:
At what page width to hide the "navigation" slot and collapse into a hamburger button.
Accepts both numbers (interpreted as px) and CSS lengths (e.g. `50em`), which are resolved based on the root element.

navigation-placement.description: Where to place the navigation when in the mobile viewport.

nav-open.description: Whether or not the navigation drawer is open. Note, the navigation drawer is only "open" on mobile views.

view.description:
The view is a reflection of the "mobileBreakpoint", when the page is larger than the `mobile-breakpoint` (768px by
default), it is considered to be a "desktop" view. The view is merely a way to distinguish when to show/hide the
navigation. You can use additional media queries to make other adjustments to content as necessary.
The default is "desktop" because the "mobile navigation drawer" isn't accessible via SSR due to drawer requiring JS.

slots.aside: Content to be shown on the right side of the page. Typically contains a table of contents, ads, etc. This section "sticks" to the top as the page scrolls.
slots.banner: The banner that gets display above the header. The banner will not be shown if no content is provided.
slots.footer: The content to display in the footer. This is always displayed underneath the viewport so will always make the page "scrollable".
slots.header: The header to display at the top of the page. If a banner is present, the header will appear below the banner. The header will not be shown if there is no content.
slots.main-footer: Footer to display inline below the main content.
slots.main-header: Header to display inline above the main content.
slots.menu: The left side of the page. If you slot an element in here, you will override the default `navigation` slot and will be handling navigation on your own. This also will not disable the fallback behavior of the navigation button. This section "sticks" to the top as the page scrolls.
slots.navigation: The main content to display in the navigation area. This is displayed on the left side of the page, if `menu` is not used. This section "sticks" to the top as the page scrolls.
slots.navigation-footer: The footer for a navigation area. On mobile this will be the footer for `<wa-drawer>`.
slots.navigation-header: The header for a navigation area. On mobile this will be the header for `<wa-drawer>`.
slots.navigation-toggle: Use this slot to slot in your own button + icon for toggling the navigation drawer. By default it is a `<wa-button>` + a 3 bars `<wa-icon>`
slots.navigation-toggle-icon: Use this to slot in your own icon for toggling the navigation drawer. By default it is 3 bars `<wa-icon>`.
slots.skip-to-content: The "skip to content" slot. You can override this If you would like to override the `Skip to content` button and add additional "Skip to X", they can be inserted here.
slots.subheader: A subheader to display below the `header`. This is a good place to put things like breadcrumbs.

distance.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the popover away from its target.

for.description: The ID of the popover's anchor element. This must be an interactive/focusable element such as a button.

placement.description:
The preferred placement of the popover. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the popover
inside of the viewport.

skidding.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the popover along its target.

without-arrow.description: Removes the arrow from the popover.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the popover has hidden and all animations are complete.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the popover has shown and all animations are complete.

wa-hide.description: Emitted when the popover begins to hide. Canceling this event will stop the popover from hiding.

wa-show.description: Emitted when the popover begins to show. Canceling this event will stop the popover from showing.

slots.(default): The popover's content. Interactive elements such as buttons and links are supported.

active.description:
Activates the positioning logic and shows the popup. When this attribute is removed, the positioning logic is torn
down and the popup will be hidden.

anchor.description:
The element the popup will be anchored to. If the anchor lives outside of the popup, you can provide the anchor
element `id`, a DOM element reference, or a `VirtualElement`. If the anchor lives inside the popup, use the
`anchor` slot instead.

arrow.description:
Attaches an arrow to the popup. The arrow's size and color can be customized using the `--arrow-size` and
`--arrow-color` custom properties. For additional customizations, you can also target the arrow using
`::part(arrow)` in your stylesheet.

arrow-padding.description:
The amount of padding between the arrow and the edges of the popup. If the popup has a border-radius, for example,
this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

arrow-placement.description:
The placement of the arrow. The default is `anchor`, which will align the arrow as close to the center of the
anchor as possible, considering available space and `arrow-padding`. A value of `start`, `end`, or `center` will
align the arrow to the start, end, or center of the popover instead.

auto-size.description: When set, this will cause the popup to automatically resize itself to prevent it from overflowing.

autoSizeBoundary.description:
The auto-size boundary describes clipping element(s) that overflow will be checked relative to when resizing. By
default, the boundary includes overflow ancestors that will cause the element to be clipped. If needed, you can
change the boundary by passing a reference to one or more elements to this property.

auto-size-padding.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the auto-size behavior will occur.

boundary.description: The bounding box to use for flipping, shifting, and auto-sizing.

distance.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel away from its anchor.

flip.description:
When set, placement of the popup will flip to the opposite site to keep it in view. You can use
`flipFallbackPlacements` to further configure how the fallback placement is determined.

flipBoundary.description:
The flip boundary describes clipping element(s) that overflow will be checked relative to when flipping. By
default, the boundary includes overflow ancestors that will cause the element to be clipped. If needed, you can
change the boundary by passing a reference to one or more elements to this property.

flip-fallback-placements.description:
If the preferred placement doesn't fit, popup will be tested in these fallback placements until one fits. Must be a
string of any number of placements separated by a space, e.g. "top bottom left". If no placement fits, the flip
fallback strategy will be used instead.

flip-fallback-strategy.description:
When neither the preferred placement nor the fallback placements fit, this value will be used to determine whether
the popup should be positioned using the best available fit based on available space or as it was initially
preferred.

flip-padding.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the flip behavior will occur.

hover-bridge.description:
When a gap exists between the anchor and the popup element, this option will add a "hover bridge" that fills the
gap using an invisible element. This makes listening for events such as `mouseenter` and `mouseleave` more sane
because the pointer never technically leaves the element. The hover bridge will only be drawn when the popover is
active.

placement.description:
The preferred placement of the popup. Note that the actual placement will vary as configured to keep the
panel inside of the viewport.

shift.description: Moves the popup along the axis to keep it in view when clipped.

shiftBoundary.description:
The shift boundary describes clipping element(s) that overflow will be checked relative to when shifting. By
default, the boundary includes overflow ancestors that will cause the element to be clipped. If needed, you can
change the boundary by passing a reference to one or more elements to this property.

shift-padding.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the shift behavior will occur.

skidding.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel along its anchor.

sync.description: Syncs the popup's width or height to that of the anchor element.

wa-reposition.description: Emitted when the popup is repositioned. This event can fire a lot, so avoid putting expensive operations in your listener or consider debouncing it.

slots.anchor: The element the popup will be anchored to. If the anchor lives outside of the popup, you can use the `anchor` attribute or property instead.

reposition.description: Forces the popup to recalculate and reposition itself.

indeterminate.description: When true, percentage is ignored, the label is hidden, and the progress bar is drawn in an indeterminate state.

label.description: A custom label for assistive devices.

value.description: The current progress as a percentage, 0 to 100.

slots.(default): A label to show inside the progress indicator.

label.description: A custom label for assistive devices.

value.description: The current progress as a percentage, 0 to 100.

slots.(default): A label to show inside the ring.

background.description: The background color. This can be any valid CSS color or `transparent`. It cannot be a CSS custom property.

error-correction.description: The level of error correction to use. [Learn more](https://www.qrcode.com/en/about/error_correction.html)

fill.description: The fill color. This can be any valid CSS color, but not a CSS custom property.

label.description: The label for assistive devices to announce. If unspecified, the value will be used instead.

radius.description: The edge radius of each module. Must be between 0 and 0.5.

size.description: The size of the QR code, in pixels.

form.description: The string pointing to a form's id.

size.description:
The radio's size. When used inside a radio group, the size will be determined by the radio group's size so this
attribute can typically be omitted.

value.description: The radio's value. When selected, the radio group will receive this value.

blur.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

disabled.description: Disables the radio group and all child radios.

hint.description: The radio groups's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

label.description:
The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot
instead.

name.description: The name of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

orientation.description: The orientation in which to show radio items.

required.description: Ensures a child radio is checked before allowing the containing form to submit.

size.description: The radio group's size. This size will be applied to all child radios and radio buttons, except when explicitly overridden.

value.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

with-hint.description: Used for SSR. if true, will show slotted hint on initial render.

with-label.description: Used for SSR. if true, will show slotted label on initial render.

change.description: Emitted when the radio group's selected value changes.

input.description: Emitted when the radio group receives user input.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.(default): The default slot where `<wa-radio>` elements are placed.
slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the radio group. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.
slots.label: The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

focus.description: Sets focus on the radio group.

getSymbol.description:
A function that customizes the symbol to be rendered. The first and only argument is the rating's current value.
The function should return a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at the specified value. Works
well with `<wa-icon>` elements.

label.description: A label that describes the rating to assistive devices.

precision.description:
The precision at which the rating will increase and decrease. For example, to allow half-star ratings, set this
attribute to `0.5`.

change.description: Emitted when the rating's value changes.

wa-hover.description: Emitted when the user hovers over a value. The `phase` property indicates when hovering starts, moves to a new value, or ends. The `value` property tells what the rating's value would be if the user were to commit to the hovered value.

focus.description: Sets focus on the rating.

date.description:
The date from which to calculate time from. If not set, the current date and time will be used. When passing a
string, it's strongly recommended to use the ISO 8601 format to ensure timezones are handled correctly. To convert
a date to this format in JavaScript, use [`date.toISOString()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toISOString).

numeric.description:
When `auto`, values such as "yesterday" and "tomorrow" will be shown when possible. When `always`, values such as
"1 day ago" and "in 1 day" will be shown.

sync.description: Keep the displayed value up to date as time passes.

wa-resize.description: Emitted when the element is resized.

slots.(default): One or more elements to watch for resizing.
The content to show inside the scroller.

form.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

hint.description: The select's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

label.description: The select's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

max-options-visible.description:
The maximum number of selected options to show when `multiple` is true. After the maximum, "+n" will be shown to
indicate the number of additional items that are selected. Set to 0 to remove the limit.

multiple.description: Allows more than one option to be selected.

name.description: The name of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

open.description:
Indicates whether or not the select is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the menu, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the select's open state.

pill.description: Draws a pill-style select with rounded edges.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the select is empty.

placement.description:
The preferred placement of the select's menu. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the listbox
inside of the viewport.

value.description: The select's value. This will be a string for single select or an array for multi-select.

with-clear.description: Adds a clear button when the select is not empty.

with-hint.description: Used for SSR purposes when hint is slotted in. Will show the hint on first render.

with-label.description: Used for SSR purposes when a label is slotted in. Will show the label on first render.

blur.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

change.description: Emitted when the control's value changes.

input.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the select's menu closes and all animations are complete.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the select's menu opens and all animations are complete.

wa-clear.description: Emitted when the control's value is cleared.

wa-hide.description: Emitted when the select's menu closes.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

wa-show.description: Emitted when the select's menu opens.

slots.(default): The listbox options. Must be `<wa-option>` elements. You can use `<wa-divider>` to group items visually.
slots.clear-icon: An icon to use in lieu of the default clear icon.
slots.end: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed at the end of the combobox.
slots.expand-icon: The icon to show when the control is expanded and collapsed. Rotates on open and close.
slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.
slots.label: The input's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.
slots.start: An element, such as `<wa-icon>`, placed at the start of the combobox.

focus.description: Sets focus on the control.

effect.description: Determines which effect the skeleton will use.

autofocus.description: Tells the browser to focus the slider when the page loads or a dialog is shown.

form.description:
The form to associate this control with. If omitted, the closest containing `<form>` will be used. The value of
this attribute must be an ID of a form in the same document or shadow root.

hint.description: The slider hint. If you need to display HTML, use the hint slot instead.

indicator-offset.description: The starting value from which to draw the slider's fill, which is based on its current value.

label.description: The slider's label. If you need to provide HTML in the label, use the `label` slot instead.

max-value.description: The maximum value of a range selection. Used only when range attribute is set.

min-value.description: The minimum value of a range selection. Used only when range attribute is set.

name.description: The name of the slider. This will be submitted with the form as a name/value pair.

range.description: Converts the slider to a range slider with two thumbs.

readonly.description: Makes the slider a read-only field.

required.description: Makes the slider a required field.

step.description: The granularity the value must adhere to when incrementing and decrementing.

tooltip-distance.description: The distance of the tooltip from the slider's thumb.

tooltip-placement.description: The placement of the tooltip in reference to the slider's thumb.

value.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

with-markers.description: Draws markers at each step along the slider.

with-tooltip.description: Draws a tooltip above the thumb when the control has focus or is dragged.

blur.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

change.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user.

input.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute. instead.
slots.label: The slider label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.
slots.reference: One or more reference labels to show visually below the slider.

focus.description: Sets focus to the slider.

stepDown.description:
Decreases the slider's value by `step`. This is a programmatic change, so `input` and `change` events will not be
emitted when this is called.

stepUp.description:
Increases the slider's value by `step`. This is a programmatic change, so `input` and `change` events will not be
emitted when this is called.

disabled.description: Disables resizing. Note that the position may still change as a result of resizing the host element.

position.description:
The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge as a percentage 0-100. Defaults to 50% of the
container's initial size.

position-in-pixels.description: The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge in pixels.

primary.description:
If no primary panel is designated, both panels will resize proportionally when the host element is resized. If a
primary panel is designated, it will maintain its size and the other panel will grow or shrink as needed when the
host element is resized.

snap.description:
One or more space-separated values at which the divider should snap. Values can be in pixels or percentages, e.g.
`"100px 50%"`.

snap-threshold.description: How close the divider must be to a snap point until snapping occurs.

wa-reposition.description: Emitted when the divider's position changes.

slots.divider: The divider. Useful for slotting in a custom icon that renders as a handle.
slots.end: Content to place in the end panel.
slots.start: Content to place in the start panel.

form.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

hint.description: The switch's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

checked.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

name.description: The name of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

required.description: Makes the switch a required field.

value.description: The value of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

with-hint.description: Used for SSR. If you slot in hint, make sure to add `with-hint` to your component to get it to properly render with SSR.

blur.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

change.description: Emitted when the control's checked state changes.

input.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the switch. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.

click.description: Simulates a click on the switch.

focus.description: Sets focus on the switch.

disabled.description: Disables the tab and prevents selection.

panel.description: The name of the tab panel this tab is associated with. The panel must be located in the same tab group.

activation.description:
When set to auto, navigating tabs with the arrow keys will instantly show the corresponding tab panel. When set to
manual, the tab will receive focus but will not show until the user presses spacebar or enter.

without-scroll-controls.description: Disables the scroll arrows that appear when tabs overflow.

wa-tab-hide.description: Emitted when a tab is hidden.

wa-tab-show.description: Emitted when a tab is shown.

slots.(default): Used for grouping tab panels in the tab group. Must be `<wa-tab-panel>` elements.
slots.nav: Used for grouping tabs in the tab group. Must be `<wa-tab>` elements. Note that `<wa-tab>` will set this slot on itself automatically.

active.description: When true, the tab panel will be shown.

pill.description: Draws a pill-style tag with rounded edges.

variant.description: The tag's theme variant. Defaults to `neutral` if not within another element with a variant.

with-remove.description: Makes the tag removable and shows a remove button.

wa-remove.description: Emitted when the remove button is activated.

autocapitalize.description: Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered by the user.

autocomplete.description:
Specifies what permission the browser has to provide assistance in filling out form field values. Refer to
[this page on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete) for available values.

autocorrect.description: Indicates whether the browser's autocorrect feature is on or off.

autofocus.description: Indicates that the input should receive focus on page load.

enterkeyhint.description: Used to customize the label or icon of the Enter key on virtual keyboards.

form.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

hint.description: The textarea's hint. If you need to display HTML, use the `hint` slot instead.

inputmode.description:
Tells the browser what type of data will be entered by the user, allowing it to display the appropriate virtual
keyboard on supportive devices.

label.description: The textarea's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

maxlength.description: The maximum length of input that will be considered valid.

minlength.description: The minimum length of input that will be considered valid.

name.description: The name of the textarea, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the input is empty.

required.description: Makes the textarea a required field.

resize.description: Controls how the textarea can be resized.

rows.description: The number of rows to display by default.

spellcheck.description: Enables spell checking on the textarea.

value.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

with-hint.description: Used for SSR. If you're slotting in a `hint` element, make sure to set this to `true`.

with-label.description: Used for SSR. If you're slotting in a `label` element, make sure to set this to `true`.

blur.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

focus.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

change.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user.

input.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

wa-invalid.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.hint: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `hint` attribute.
slots.label: The textarea's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

focus.description: Sets focus on the textarea.

scrollPosition.signature: scrollPosition(position?: { top?: number; left?: number }) => { top: number; left: number } | undefined
scrollPosition.description: Gets or sets the textarea's scroll position.

select.description: Selects all the text in the textarea.

setRangeText.signature: setRangeText(replacement: string, start?: number, end?: number, selectMode: 'select' | 'start' | 'end' | 'preserve' = 'preserve') => void
setRangeText.description: Replaces a range of text with a new string.

setSelectionRange.signature: setSelectionRange(selectionStart: number, selectionEnd: number, selectionDirection: 'forward' | 'backward' | 'none' = 'none') => void
setSelectionRange.description: Sets the start and end positions of the text selection (0-based).

disabled.description: Disables the tooltip so it won't show when triggered.

distance.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the tooltip away from its target.

hide-delay.description: The amount of time to wait before hiding the tooltip when the user mouses out..

open.description: Indicates whether or not the tooltip is open. You can use this in lieu of the show/hide methods.

placement.description:
The preferred placement of the tooltip. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the tooltip
inside of the viewport.

show-delay.description: The amount of time to wait before showing the tooltip when the user mouses in.

skidding.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the tooltip along its target.

trigger.description:
Controls how the tooltip is activated. Possible options include `click`, `hover`, `focus`, and `manual`. Multiple
options can be passed by separating them with a space. When manual is used, the tooltip must be activated
programmatically.

without-arrow.description: Removes the arrow from the tooltip.

wa-after-hide.description: Emitted after the tooltip has hidden and all animations are complete.

wa-after-show.description: Emitted after the tooltip has shown and all animations are complete.

wa-hide.description: Emitted when the tooltip begins to hide.

wa-show.description: Emitted when the tooltip begins to show.

slots.(default): The tooltip's default slot where any content should live. Interactive content should be avoided.

selection.description:
The selection behavior of the tree. Single selection allows only one node to be selected at a time. Multiple
displays checkboxes and allows more than one node to be selected. Leaf allows only leaf nodes to be selected.

wa-selection-change.description: Emitted when a tree item is selected or deselected.

slots.collapse-icon: The icon to show when the tree item is collapsed. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.
slots.expand-icon: The icon to show when the tree item is expanded. Works best with `<wa-icon>`.

selected.description: Draws the tree item in a selected state.

wa-after-collapse.description: Emitted after the tree item collapses and all animations are complete.

wa-after-expand.description: Emitted after the tree item expands and all animations are complete.

wa-collapse.description: Emitted when the tree item collapses.

wa-expand.description: Emitted when the tree item expands.

wa-lazy-change.description: Emitted when the tree item's lazy state changes.

wa-lazy-load.description: Emitted when a lazy item is selected. Use this event to asynchronously load data and append items to the tree before expanding. After appending new items, remove the `lazy` attribute to remove the loading state and update the tree.

slots.collapse-icon: The icon to show when the tree item is collapsed.
slots.expand-icon: The icon to show when the tree item is expanded.

getChildrenItems.signature: getChildrenItems({ includeDisabled = true }: { includeDisabled?: boolean } = {}) => WaTreeItem[]
getChildrenItems.description: Gets all the nested tree items in this node.

src.description: The URL of the content to display.

zoom.description: The current zoom of the frame, e.g. 0 = 0% and 1 = 100%.

zoom-levels.description: The zoom levels to step through when using zoom controls. This does not restrict programmatic changes to the zoom.

error.description: Emitted from the internal iframe when it fails to load.

load.description: Emitted when the internal iframe when it finishes loading.

slots.zoom-in-icon: The slot that contains the zoom in icon.
slots.zoom-out-icon: The slot that contains the zoom out icon.

zoomIn.description: Zooms in to the next available zoom level.

zoomOut.description: Zooms out to the previous available zoom level.
